title: STANDARD FOR DRIED ROOTS, RHIZOMES AND BULBS: DRIED OR DEHYDRATED GINGER CXS 343-2021

sections.section: STANDARD FOR DRIED ROOTS, RHIZOMES AND BULBS: DRIED OR DEHYDRATED GINGER CXS 343-2021
sections.summary: Document title and adoption note: Standard for Dried Roots, Rhizomes and Bulbs – Dried or Dehydrated Ginger (CXS 343-2021), adopted 2021 and amended in 2022 with an amendment table describing changes from CAC45 (Dec 2022).

Applies to plant products in dried or dehydrated form as spices (defined in Section 2.1) offered for direct consumption, as ingredients, or for repackaging, and excludes products intended for industrial processing.
Describes dried/dehydrated ginger: product definition (rhizomes listed in Table 1) and permissible styles/forms—whole, pieces (cut/diced/sliced) and ground/powdered.
Defines dried or dehydrated ginger as the product obtained from the rhizomes of Zingiber officinale Roscoe (see Table 1).
Permits the following styles/forms for dried ginger: whole (single/branched rhizomes), pieces (cut/diced/sliced), and ground/powdered.

Sets essential composition and quality: product must meet composition requirements in Annexes I and II and possess characteristic odour, flavour and colour free from foreign taints (notably rancidity and mustiness), with allowed defects not impairing appearance, keeping quality or presentation.
States that dried or dehydrated ginger shall conform to the composition requirements specified in Annexes I and II.

Specifies quality factors: characteristic sensory properties (odour, flavour, colour) without foreign taints and compliance with chemical and physical requirements in Annex I (Table 2) and Annex II (Table 3), with defects allowed only if they do not affect general appearance, keeping quality or presentation.

Requires the product to have a characteristic odour, flavour and colour (which may vary by geo‑climatic factors) and to be free from foreign odour, flavour or colour, especially rancidity and mustiness.

Requires compliance with chemical characteristics in Annex I (Table 2) and physical characteristics in Annex II (Table 3), and that allowed defects must not affect appearance, keeping quality or presentation.

Specifies permitted food additives and processing aids: anticaking agents listed in CXS 192-1995 for powdered products, bleaching with sulphur dioxide (see Table) and processing aids consistent with CXG 75-2010 (e.g., calcium oxide up to 2.5% dry basis).
Allows sulphur dioxide (INS 220) as a bleaching agent with a maximum residual SO2 level of 150 mg/kg.
Allows sulphur dioxide (INS 220) as a bleaching agent with a maximum residual SO2 level of 150 mg/kg.
Processing aids must comply with the Guidelines on Substances used as Processing Aids (CXG 75-2010); lists calcium oxide (INS 529) with a maximum level of 2.5% on a dry basis.

Products must comply with Codex contaminant and toxin limits (CXS 193-1995), the Code of Practice for Prevention/Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices (CXC 78-2017), and Codex pesticide maximum residue limits.

Recommends preparation and handling according to General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Low‑Moisture Foods (CXC 75-2015) including Annex III on spices, and compliance with microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.
Requires containers to be as full as practicable without impairing quality and consistent with proper declaration of contents.

Requires labelling in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) with specific provisions: product name per Section 2.1, optional style/variety/trade name, mandatory country of origin (harvest/region/year optional), and non‑retail container labelling per CXS 346-2021.
For compliance, methods of analysis and sampling in the Recommended Methods (CXS 234-1999) relevant to this standard shall be used; a sampling plan is noted as to be developed.
Requires that methods of analysis and sampling specified in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to this standard be used for compliance checking.
Placeholder heading indicating the sampling plan is to be developed; no substantive sampling requirements are provided.

Annex I contains Table 3 specifying maximum limits for physical characteristics and contaminants (extraneous/foreign matter %, whole and live insect counts per 100 g, mammalian and other excreta mg/kg, and mould/insect-defiled percentages) for dried or dehydrated ginger by form (whole, pieces, ground), with 'N/A' indicating the form was not evaluated for that provision.
Annex II heading only; no substantive content provided.
